goal: Remove the closed runtime patch permit-consume cluster identified by BPK-232 while preserving real safety, write, provider, runtime dry-run, and activation paths.
scope_out: Do not change permitApply, server routes, runtime dry-run routes, builder safety policy, local safety guard, providers, activation locks, env, DB, deploy, package files, workflows, or old audit contracts.
Do not delete by broad shell discovery beyond the BPK-232 checked cluster.
claims: BPK-232 found the cluster has 41 source files, 41 direct tests, no external source importers, no external test importers, no server route mounts, and no bpk-governance-manifest refs.
Deleting the checked cluster should not affect retained live paths.
Typecheck and full tests are the backstop for hidden imports or dynamic references.
assumptions: Historical contracts and review packets remain as audit history.
The BPK-232 branch is included in this stack so the dependency map is reviewable with the deletion.
dod: The 41 checked runtime patch/server patch source files are deleted.
The 41 direct matching tests are deleted.
Retained safety/write/provider/runtime dry-run files are untouched.
Typecheck, full builder tests, task-lock verify, and diff-check pass.
stop_conditions: Typecheck reports a dangling import outside the deleted cluster.
Full tests fail outside the deleted cluster.
A route, provider, write, runtime dry-run, activation, env, DB, deploy, package, workflow, or old audit-contract change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-232 mapped the runtime patch permit-consume cluster as a closed island: 41 source files, 41 direct tests, zero external source/test importers, zero server route mounts, and zero governance-manifest refs.
